regime_justification: USDJPY stable near 158.50, BOJ hawkish signaling June rate hike, JGB yields at 27-year highs, oil elevated near $105/bbl on Hormuz closure, VIX calm but MOVE rising, intervention risk high—mixed signals warrant watch-list status.

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY near 158.5 with BOJ Summary of Opinions signaling June hike, JGB 10Y at 2.7% (highest since 1997), MOF already intervened once in early May (2 more windows allowed by IMF rules), Japan PPI 4.9% accelerating inflation pressure.

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: BOJ June 16 meeting 3 weeks away; hawkish signals building, JGB yields spiking, yen intervention risk elevated but USDJPY stable for now.

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: BOJ meeting approaches; any hawkish Ueda remarks or rate-check rumors could compress spread and trigger vol spike before June 16 decision.

3w.label: Likely
3w.driver: BOJ meeting window (June 16); 6-3 split vote in April, Summary of Opinions signals 'quite possible' June hike, JGB at cycle high—unwind signatures present.

4w.label: Watchful
4w.driver: Post-BOJ resolution scenario; if hike delivered unwind begins, if hold then reprieve until next meeting—grade falls back as catalyst passes.

bottom_line: Stage 2 Pre-Intervention regime persists: USDJPY stable near 158.5 but BOJ board signals June hike ('quite possible'), JGB 10Y hit 2.7% (highest since 1997), Japan PPI 4.9% y/y accelerating. MOF already intervened once in early May and has 2 more IMF-compliant windows before November. Oil $105/bbl on Hormuz closure fuels imported inflation (¥/bbl ~16,650), US 10Y 4.6%, spread 187 bps still wide but JGB rising faster than UST. VIX 18, MOVE 70—equity vol calm but rates vol rising. BTC $79k range-bound, XRP $1.45. Grades watchful (C) 1-2 weeks, likely (B) week 3 around BOJ meeting June 16, then back to watchful (C) week 4 post-resolution. Next 3 weeks are the highest-risk window for carry unwind catalyst since intervention commenced.

MOF intervened early May (1 of 3 IMF-compliant windows used), 2 more windows available before Nov, USDJPY 158.5 near watch line, Katayama-Bessent May 12 meeting discussed FX markets

trigger_map.trigger: JGB 10Y break above prior cycle high
trigger_map.note: JGB 10Y 2.7% exceeded 2.55% prior April high, now highest since 1997, bid-to-cover 3.9 (highest since Sept), Japan PPI 4.9% y/y accelerating—active breakout

trigger_map.trigger: Energy / Hormuz shock pushing ¥/bbl > prior peak
trigger_map.note: WTI $105/bbl, ¥/bbl ¥16,607 near peak levels, Hormuz closure cut 12.8M bpd supply (80% reduction), Iran-US talks stalled, Trump threatened 'annihilation'—ongoing shock
MOVE 70, rising from 69.6 but below 100 threshold; JGB spike and Fed hike pricing pushing rates vol higher, setup for regime change if crosses 100

narrative.p1: USDJPY stable overnight near 158.5 (+0.09%) but pressure building beneath the surface. Japan released April PPI at 4.9% y/y (vs 2.9% prior), the fastest producer inflation since May 2023, driven by surging energy costs from the Hormuz closure. JGB 10Y spiked 5 bps to 2.7%, the highest since 1997, as markets price in growing BOJ hawkishness. BOJ board member Masu stated the bank should raise rates 'as soon as possible' and the Summary of Opinions released May 12 indicated 'it is quite possible' the board will hike at the June 16 meeting. No stage change from yesterday (Stage 2 Pre-Intervention persists), but the Jun 16 catalyst window is now 3 weeks away and the Setup is tightening.
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US-JP 10Y spread 190 bps (1.90 ppt), down 9 bps as JGB rises faster than UST. Carry-to-vol proxy unavailable (USDJPY 1M implied vol data not retrieved). USDJPY 5-day pace +0.4% (orderly), 60-day mean ~157.8 so spot 158.5 is neutral/slightly stretched. ¥/bbl crude ¥16,607 (WTI $105 × 158.5), driving import-cost inflation that BOJ cannot ignore. Stage 2 Pre-Intervention: USDJPY near MOF watch line, BOJ rhetoric shifted hawkish, JGB at cycle high (trigger firing), intervention already occurred once in early May (MOF has 2 more IMF-compliant windows before Nov per Bloomberg/Japan Times May 5). 1-2 week grades C (watchful): data-driven, setup fragile but no immediate trigger. Week 3 grade B (likely): BOJ meeting June 16 is the catalyst—6-3 split vote in April, hawkish Summary of Opinions, JGB breakout in progress. Applied unwind prior: would grade week 3 as C without the prior, but BOJ meeting + JGB spike + intervention constraint + oil shock stack up to justify B. Week 4 grade C: scenario-weighted drop back to watchful as catalyst resolves (either hike delivered and unwind begins, or hold and reprieve until next meeting).
narrative.p3: BTC $79k range-bound, rejected 200-day MA $82,228 for the fifth time this month. Powell-Warsh Fed transition uncertainty (Powell's term ended May 15, Warsh took over) and rising Treasury yields (US 10Y 4.6%, highest in a year) pressure non-yielding assets. BTC up +1.5% on week but no breakout—crypto liquidity sympathy muted. XRP $1.45, stabilized in $1.40-$1.50 range since Feb, 78% probability trades above $1.50 in May per Kalshi prediction markets, awaiting CLARITY Act Senate Banking Committee review mid-May. Crypto positioning: risk-on rotation stalled; BTC needs weekly close above $82k to confirm the historical Powell-exit pattern has broken (every prior Fed Chair transition crashed BTC ~82%, but Warsh disclosed $100M+ crypto holdings). Until that close, crypto is a follower not a leader—watch for BTC to confirm or deny broader risk appetite, not drive it.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: Weeks 1-2 graded C (watchful): USDJPY stable near 158.5, spread still 190 bps, VIX 18 (calm), no immediate catalyst. BOJ meeting 3 weeks away. Risk: any Ueda/Masu hawkish remarks, MOF rate-check rumors, or JGB spike above 2.8% could compress spread and spike vol before the meeting. Data-driven grade reflects current conditions, not the Jun 16 catalyst loading in week 3.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: Grade rises from C to B at week 3: BOJ meeting June 16 is the pivot. April meeting split 6-3 (Takata/Tamura/Nakagawa dissented for 1.0% hike), Summary of Opinions stated 'quite possible' June hike, JGB 10Y 2.7% (highest since 1997), Japan PPI 4.9% y/y accelerating. Early unwind signatures present: JGB breakout firing, oil shock feeding inflation, MOF already intervened once (2 windows left). Applied unwind prior as tiebreaker—data genuinely mixed (USDJPY stable, VIX low) but BOJ catalyst + JGB spike tips to B.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: Grade drops back from B to C at week 4: scenario-weighted resolution. If BOJ hikes June 16, unwind begins (spread compresses, yen rallies, carry liquidates)—but that starts the unwind, doesn't sustain high alert into week 4 unless cascade triggers. If BOJ holds, market reprieve until next meeting (likely July). Non-monotonic grade path valid: catalyst sits in week 3, resolves by week 4. Week 4 C reflects post-meeting environment, not sustained B-level stress unless financial contagion emerges.

watch_list: BOJ Governor Ueda remarks or board member speeches before June 16—any hawkish tone could spike JGB/USDJPY vol and trigger early positioning
MOF rate-check rumors or Katayama comments on FX intervention—MOF has 2 IMF-compliant windows left, market watching for signals
JGB 10Y yield crossing 2.8% or 3.0%—would mark further cycle high breakout and accelerate carry unwind fears
WTI crude breaking above $110/bbl or Hormuz reopening headlines—oil shock is the primary inflation driver for Japan, any resolution or escalation moves the needle
US Treasury 10Y yield crossing 4.75% or Fed speakers signaling hike—widening spread gives carry reprieve, but higher UST yields pressure risk assets globally
